summary: Frontend testing for Mira OS bug fixes: Services tab, Inbox notifications, and Ticket reply functionality

minor.issue: Intermittent 'Failed to fetch pets' error in console - appears to be timing/network related

ui_bugs.issue: Modal blocks access to Services panel content - makes it difficult to test timely service clicks

action_items: Main fix verified: SERVICE_CONFIGS now includes vet-consult, health-checkup, vaccination, wellness, dental-care, lab-tests - fallback changed from grooming to general
Main fix verified: TicketThread multi-endpoint fetching working - tickets load from /api/mira/tickets endpoint
Ticket reply functionality working correctly with optimistic UI
Consider adding a dismiss button or auto-close for Test Scenarios modal after first visit
context_for_next_testing_agent: Testing done on mira-demo Services tab, Inbox notifications, and Ticket reply. Services panel shows timely services correctly (console logs confirm [SERVICES SOUL] context with health, food, grooming, travel). TicketThread loads without 'Ticket not found' error. Reply input and send button work with optimistic UI. Test credentials: dipali@clubconcierge.in / test123

services_tab_fix.evidence: SERVICE_CONFIGS in ServiceRequestBuilder.jsx includes comprehensive health services: vet-consult (line 77), health-checkup (line 83), vaccination (line 89), wellness (line 95), dental-care (line 101), lab-tests (line 107). Fallback changed to 'general' on line 263
services_tab_fix.console_output: [SERVICES SOUL] Timely context: [health, food, grooming, travel]

inbox_ticket_not_found_fix.evidence: TicketThread loads successfully. Console shows: [TicketThread] Found ticket at: /api/mira/tickets/ADV-20260221-0013

ticket_reply_functionality.evidence: Reply input field (data-testid=reply-input) found, send button works, optimistic UI shows message immediately with count increasing from 3 to 4

inbox_yellow_color.evidence: InboxRow uses gradient colors for pillar styling (orange-500/amber-500 for Dine, emerald-500/teal-500 for Care). Text is white on colored backgrounds. The amber-500 color is only used on 'Mark as Unread' swipe action button with white text
